mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 3.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, increasing recession risks and depressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal tightening monetary policy against persistent price pressures, creating headwinds for equity earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS signal remains < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) growth indicates robust corporate sector activity, boosting earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high INDPRO regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction remains < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals economic contraction, increasing risk aversion and depressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low GDP regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening, raising borrowing costs and reducing corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
